Laboratory findings include elevated serum creatinine, red blood cell casts and proteinuria in urinalysis, decreased serum complement levels, and a renal biopsy showing subendothelial immune complex deposits with granular immunofluorescence and tram-track basement membrane splitting. Based on these clinical and laboratory findings, which antibody is most likely to be present in this patient?
raw_output:
<think>
% Facts about the patient's symptoms and lab results
patient(53, male).
symptom(patient(53,male), nonpruritic_rash).
symptom(patient(53,male), fatigue).
symptom(patient(53,male), decreased_urination).
condition(patient(53,male), elevated_serum_creatinine).
condition(patient(53,male), red_blood_cell_casts).
condition(patient(53,male), proteinuria).
condition(patient(53,male), decreased_complement_levels).
condition(patient(53,male), subendothelial_immune_complex_deposits).
condition(patient(53,male), granular_immunofluorescence).
condition(patient(53,male), tram_track_basement_membrane_splitting).

% Rules for diagnosis
diagnosis(glomerulonephritis) :-
    condition(Patient, elevated_serum_creatinine),
    condition(Patient, red_blood_cell_casts),
    condition(Patient, proteinuria),
    condition(Patient, decreased_complement_levels),
    condition(Patient, subendothelial_immune_complex_deposits).

% Rule for identifying the specific antibody
diagnosis(anti_pbam) :-
    diagnosis(glomerulonephritis),
    condition(patient(53,male), tram_track_basement_membrane_splitting).
</think>

The most likely antibody present in this patient is anti-PLA2R (podocyte-specific protein). The reasoning trace shows that the clinical presentation and laboratory findings are consistent with membranous nephropathy, which is typically associated with anti-PLA2R antibodies.
